name: Caido Web Security Testing API
what_it_does: Modern web security testing proxy with REST and GraphQL API for automating security testing workflows — enabling agents to send crafted requests, replay traffic, run HTTPQL queries on captured traffic, and manage testing projects programmatically.
use_cases: Automating web application security scans by replaying and mutating captured traffic
Querying HTTP traffic history with HTTPQL for security analysis agents
Intercepting and modifying requests in automated security testing pipelines
Managing security testing projects and replays via API for CI/CD integration
Building custom security automation on top of Caido's plugin SDK
not_for: Unauthenticated scanning at scale (use Nuclei or Nessus for that)
Passive network monitoring without proxied traffic
Organizations that need enterprise DAST without local proxy setup
best_when: A security engineer or agent needs to automate web security testing with a modern proxy that has API access for programmatic traffic manipulation.
avoid_when: You need a fully managed DAST/SAST solution without local proxy infrastructure — use Checkmarx or Veracode.

auth.notes: API key for local Caido instance. No cloud authentication — Caido is a local proxy tool. API key passed as header. GraphQL API for complex queries on traffic data.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free community edition with core features
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro $8/month with advanced features and cloud sync
pricing.notes: Affordable pricing compared to Burp Suite Pro. Community edition is feature-rich for individual researchers. Pro adds collaboration, cloud backup, and advanced automation features.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation covers GraphQL and REST APIs. HTTPQL query language for traffic filtering is powerful but has learning curve. Plugin SDK enables custom automation. Local-only API reduces attack surface.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Traffic queries are idempotent reads. Replay operations create new requests — not idempotent by nature. Project management operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Local proxy setup required — agents must configure target application to use Caido as proxy
HTTPQL is a custom query language — learning curve before traffic querying is efficient
Plugin SDK is TypeScript-only — custom automation limited to TypeScript
Traffic history is local — not accessible remotely without VPN or tunnel

security_score_components.security_notes: Local-only API means minimal attack surface. TLS optional (local network). API key authentication. Open-source community focus. No external data transmission. Tool designed for authorized security testing only.
